creator: jah@FreeBSD.org
text:
Apologies for the delay, here are the (e)cap reports for all DMAR units on the system:

Apr  7 19:01:39 corona kernel: dmar0: <DMA remap> iomem 0xbb7fc000-0xbb7fcfff on acpi0

Apr  7 19:01:39 corona kernel: dmar0: ecap=f050df<C,QI,DI,IR,EIM,PT,SC>,600, mhmw=15, iro=80

Apr  7 19:01:39 corona kernel: dmar2: ecap=f050df<C,QI,DI,IR,EIM,PT,SC>,600, mhmw=15, iro=80

Apr  7 19:01:39 corona kernel: dmar4: regs@0xa5ffc000, ver=4.0, seg=0, flags=<1<INCLUDE_ALL_PCI>>
Apr  7 19:01:39 corona kernel: dmar4: cap=40780466<PLMR,PHMR,ZLR>,8ed008c<PSI,DWD,DRD,PSI>, ndoms=2, sagaw=4, mgaw=56, fro=64, nfr=1, superp=3, mamv=45

All appear to have the same capabilities, and all are coherent (dmar4 is the unit that hosts the NVMe drive)
